steps.title: Find the decisive evidence
steps.content:
The passage explicitly states that the alternative interpretation “accounts for all the evidence.” The evidence in question is why both Valdez and Sandoval are taped to the wall.

The women-constraints reading explains Valdez but not Sandoval; the art-establishment context explains both as non-muralist Chicano artists facing aesthetic constraints.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content:
D concludes that Instant Mural is “a reflection on the constraining aesthetic expectations placed on Chicano artists in general rather than on the social constraints placed on women specifically.” This captures the alternative context—that both Valdez and Sandoval are constrained by the art establishment's aesthetic expectations, not gender roles.

The other three reintroduce the rejected women-constraints angle (A, C) or misidentify what the establishment constrains (B).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
